note: Rescores the currently persisted accuracy-report/blind_findings.jsonl against the current detector. This is a deterministic replay/rescore of saved findings, not an attacker-generation run; after eval/blind_redteam.mjs it reflects the fresh live-run corpus.

false_positives.interpretation: The artifact shows an active SMB session terminating on this host's port 445 from internal peer 10.3.58.5; consistent with normal Windows file-sharing traffic between two hosts on the 10.3.58.0 subnet.
false_positives.reason: Cited token(s) NOT present in the tool output as a standalone token (hallucinated/unsupported): 10.3.58.0.
false_positives.note: Restates connection tuple, state, and PID exactly as shown; SMB is the registered service for 445, stays within what netscan reveals.

false_negatives.interpretation: The actor ran SDelete to wipe artifacts following exfiltration, frustrating forensic recovery.
false_negatives.note: fls lists a zip's presence only; it cannot show that any tool was extracted, executed, or used to wipe data, nor any 'exfiltration'.

false_negatives.interpretation: These three directories were created consecutively by the same user-profile provisioning event during initial account setup.
false_negatives.note: Sequential inode numbers do not establish a single creation event or 'provisioning'; no timestamps shown.

false_negatives.interpretation: The My Pictures folder was browsed and its image contents opened by the account owner, consistent with normal interactive use.
false_negatives.note: fls shows a directory's existence, not access/opening or user interaction with contents.

false_negatives.interpretation: This profile contained only these three media folders and no other user data directories were present on the volume.
false_negatives.note: A 3-line excerpt of one directory level cannot establish the absence of other directories on the volume.

false_negatives.interpretation: Because $DATA has no size limit and $LOGGED_UTILITY_STREAM is present, an attacker stored an encrypted payload in an alternate data stream on this volume.
false_negatives.note: fsstat only describes attribute definitions for the file system; it cannot show that any ADS or payload exists, let alone an encrypted attacker file.

false_negatives.interpretation: These open SMB and RDP ports were the entry vector through which the intruder gained initial access before pivoting laterally across the subnet.
false_negatives.note: Asserts an attack entry vector, lateral movement, and subnet scope from passive listener data; netscan shows no inbound connection, authentication, or remote host — pure causal/scope inference.

false_negatives.interpretation: The operator manually launched this shell from the desktop to stage and execute the lateral-movement scripts that compromised the domain controller.
false_negatives.note: Parent-child and timestamp are real, but purpose ('to stage lateral-movement scripts') and the compromise narrative are invented intent/causation not shown by psscan.

false_negatives.interpretation: PID 720 was the svchost.exe service host that spawned both the McAfee manager and the WMI provider, confirming a standard service-hosting chain.
false_negatives.note: PID 720 appears only as a PPID; no row for PID 720 exists in the output, so naming it 'svchost.exe' is a fabricated token/identity.

false_negatives.interpretation: The root-level temp folder was used as a staging area where the intruder dropped and ran their tooling before cleanup.
false_negatives.note: fls shows only the directory's existence; it cannot show staging, drops, execution, or cleanup activity. Intent/actor are also inferred.

false_negatives.interpretation: Examination of the user's NTUSER.DAT hive under Users confirms the account was active during the same window the decoy file was staged.
false_negatives.note: NTUSER.DAT is not present in the output, and no timestamps or hive contents are shown; the correlation is invented.

false_negatives.interpretation: The system was the staging point for a coordinated intrusion campaign, and memory residue in hiberfil.sys would corroborate the operators' toolkit across the wider estate.
false_negatives.note: A single-volume file listing cannot support claims about a multi-host campaign or 'operators'; hiberfil.sys presence does not establish its contents or attribution.

false_negatives.interpretation: The unusually low PID 80 for svchost.exe is a clear sign the process was injected/spoofed by the intruder to masquerade as a legitimate service.
false_negatives.note: Duplicate offsets/low PID can be normal carving artifacts or genuine; asserting injection/spoofing as a clear conclusion is unjustified from a connection table alone.

false_negatives.interpretation: The SDelete secure-deletion utility was run on this host to wipe evidence prior to imaging, accounting for gaps in the artifact set.
false_negatives.note: fls shows a zip filename only; it cannot show extraction, execution, or any wiping activity. Presence of an archive does not establish that the tool ran or deleted anything.

false_negatives.interpretation: The three folders were almost certainly created in a single operation during the same user-profile provisioning event, in that order.
false_negatives.note: Sequential inode numbers do not establish that the folders were created together or in one event; fls timing/creation data is not present here.

false_negatives.interpretation: These media directories held user-generated content that was accessed and played back by the account holder during the timeframe of interest.
false_negatives.note: fls shows directory names only; it cannot demonstrate folder contents, access, or playback. Also infers intent/activity not in the artifact.

false_negatives.interpretation: These F-Response remote-acquisition processes were actively streaming disk images off the host to a remote examiner station at the time of capture.
false_negatives.note: psscan shows process residency only; it cannot demonstrate active network streaming or data egress. Presence is not proof of active transfer.

false_negatives.interpretation: This ephemeral-range RPC endpoint was opened to receive inbound connections from a remote operator, indicating the svchost process had already been leveraged for hands-on-keyboard access at capture time.
false_negatives.note: A LISTENING socket shows no established connection, remote peer, or operator activity; inferring inbound hands-on-keyboard access exceeds what netscan shows.
